title: Remove Gemini API system and replace with local candle analysis engine

requirements.text: Remove the entire external Gemini/API integration system from the frontend. Delete or disable frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts, frontend/src/config/apiConfig.ts, and all API key validation logic. Remove the Gemini API key input field from the Settings page (frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx) and all related hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts (getGeminiApiKey, setGeminiApiKey mutations). Remove API key validation from frontend/src/pages/ProcessingScreen.tsx. Keep all other settings controls (theme, AI sensitivity, timeframe, daily operation limit) intact.
acceptanceCriteria: No Gemini API key field appears anywhere in the Settings page
No API calls to external endpoints are made during analysis
No API key validation or error messages related to missing API keys appear
frontend/src/config/apiConfig.ts is deleted or fully emptied of API configuration
All other settings (theme, timeframe, sensitivity, daily limit) remain functional

requirements.text: Remove backend Gemini API key storage from backend/main.mo. Delete the getGeminiApiKey and setGeminiApiKey functions and any associated stable storage for the API key. Ensure backward compatibility so existing analyses and other settings stored in the backend are unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo no longer contains getGeminiApiKey or setGeminiApiKey functions
Existing stored analyses and user settings are preserved
Backend compiles and deploys without errors

requirements.text: Create a local candle analysis engine in frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts that analyzes an uploaded chart image client-side without any external API calls. The engine must: (1) accept a File or Blob image input; (2) draw the image onto an HTML Canvas to read pixel data; (3) detect the candlestick chart region by scanning for alternating green/red vertical pixel columns against a dark or light background; (4) identify the last 5–7 visible candles by detecting their body color (green = bullish, red = bearish), body height, wick length, and relative position; (5) compute a next-candle probability score based on: trend direction across the detected candles (ascending tops/bottoms = ALTA, descending = BAIXA, otherwise LATERAL), last candle pattern (engulfing, hammer, shooting star, doji, pin bar detected from body-to-wick ratio and color), momentum (consecutive same-color candles increase confidence); (6) return a typed LocalAnalysisResult object containing: sinal ('COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'SEM ENTRADA'), tendencia ('ALTA' | 'BAIXA' | 'LATERAL'), confianca (number 0–100), probabilidade_alta (number 0–100), probabilidade_baixa (number 0–100), padroes (string[] in Portuguese), forca ('fraca' | 'média' | 'forte'), explicacao (one Portuguese sentence summarizing the prediction), and erro (optional string if chart region cannot be detected). The probabilities must sum to 100. If the chart region cannot be reliably detected, set erro to 'Não foi possível identificar o gráfico corretamente' and return a SEM ENTRADA signal.

quotes: deixa apenas configuração de analise de candle e do jeito que pedi

acceptanceCriteria: localCandleAnalysis.ts exists and exports an analyzeChartImage(file: File) function
Function returns a typed LocalAnalysisResult without making any HTTP requests
Detected candle count is between 5 and 7 when chart is identifiable
probabilidade_alta + probabilidade_baixa === 100 in all valid results
sinal is 'COMPRA' when probabilidade_alta >= 60, 'VENDA' when probabilidade_baixa >= 60, otherwise 'SEM ENTRADA'
Returns erro field if chart region cannot be detected from pixel data

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/types/apiTypes.ts (or create frontend/src/types/analysisTypes.ts) to define the LocalAnalysisResult interface with fields: sinal ('COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'SEM ENTRADA'), tendencia ('ALTA' | 'BAIXA' | 'LATERAL'), confianca (number), probabilidade_alta (number), probabilidade_baixa (number), padroes (string[]), forca ('fraca' | 'média' | 'forte'), explicacao (string), pontuacao (optional number), and erro (optional string). Add JSDoc comments explaining each field and note that all analysis is performed locally without external API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: LocalAnalysisResult interface is exported and used by the analysis engine and results components
All fields have correct TypeScript types
JSDoc comments are present on each field

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/pages/ProcessingScreen.tsx to use the local candle analysis engine instead of any external API. When analysis starts: (1) load the image from sessionStorage; (2) display the four sequential Portuguese stages (Carregando imagem → Processando dados → Detectando padrões → Gerando resultado) with animated indicators, advancing each stage on a short timeout to simulate processing steps; (3) call analyzeChartImage(file) from the local engine during the 'Detectando padrões' stage; (4) on success, store the result in sessionStorage and navigate to the Results page; (5) if the result contains an erro field, display the Portuguese error message 'Não foi possível identificar o gráfico corretamente' with a 'Tentar novamente' button that navigates back to AnalyzeChart; (6) remove all API key validation checks and HTTP error handling (404, 403, etc.) since no network calls are made.
acceptanceCriteria: No HTTP requests are made during the processing screen
All four Portuguese stages animate sequentially
Successful analysis navigates to Results page with LocalAnalysisResult in sessionStorage
Error state shows 'Não foi possível identificar o gráfico corretamente' with retry button
No API key validation step occurs

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/utils/mapApiResponse.ts (or create a new mapper) to map LocalAnalysisResult fields to the internal AnalysisResult format used by Results.tsx and history components. Preserve probabilidade_alta, probabilidade_baixa, forca, padroes, tendencia, confianca, sinal, and explicacao fields through the mapping so they are all available for rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: Mapper function accepts LocalAnalysisResult and returns an internal AnalysisResult
All new fields (probabilidade_alta, probabilidade_baixa, forca) are preserved
Existing Results.tsx and History components receive correctly mapped data

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display the original uploaded screenshot as a small thumbnail (approximately 80×60 px) positioned beside the main analysis output area. Retrieve the image data URL from sessionStorage using the key used during upload. Style the thumbnail with a subtle border and rounded corners consistent with the active dark/light theme. The thumbnail must be visible alongside the results, not below them, serving as a visual reference for the analyzed chart.
acceptanceCriteria: A small chart thumbnail (~80×60 px) appears beside the results output
Thumbnail is retrieved from sessionStorage and rendered as an element
Thumbnail has rounded corners and a subtle border matching the current theme
Thumbnail is visible in both dark and light mode

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display a prominent probability sentence near the top of the results, immediately below the signal card. The sentence must read in Portuguese: '[XX]% de probabilidade de ALTA / [YY]% de BAIXA na próxima vela' where XX is probabilidade_alta and YY is probabilidade_baixa from the analysis result. Style the ALTA portion in green and the BAIXA portion in red. Also show two horizontal percentage bar indicators side by side (green bar for ALTA, red bar for BAIXA) filling proportionally to their values.

quotes: frase dizendo quantos % de queda ou alta

acceptanceCriteria: Probability sentence appears prominently below the signal card
Text reads 'XX% de probabilidade de ALTA / YY% de BAIXA na próxima vela' with correct values
ALTA text and bar are styled green; BAIXA text and bar are styled red
Two horizontal percentage bars are proportionally filled
Section only renders when probabilidade_alta and probabilidade_baixa are present

requirements.text: Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display the candle analysis output fields in the following order with Portuguese labels: (1) Tendência (ALTA/BAIXA/LATERAL), (2) Padrões encontrados (list of detected patterns from padroes array), (3) Força (fraca/média/forte with color-coded badge: gray=fraca, amber=média, green=forte), (4) Confiança percentage, (5) Sinal (COMPRA ↑ / VENDA ↓ / SEM ENTRADA) with directional arrows and color coding, (6) Explicação (one-sentence Portuguese explanation from explicacao field). Ensure this order is consistent and all labels are in Portuguese (Brazil). If the result contains an erro field, display the Portuguese error message prominently instead of the results fields.

quotes: analise de candle e do jeito que pedi

acceptanceCriteria: Results fields render in the specified order: Tendência, Padrões, Força, Confiança, Sinal, Explicação
Força badge uses gray/amber/green styling for fraca/média/forte
Sinal shows ↑ for COMPRA (green), ↓ for VENDA (red), neutral for SEM ENTRADA
Explicação sentence renders below the signal
Error message displays prominently when erro field is present

requirements.text: Update the Settings page (frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx) to remove the Gemini API key section entirely and replace it with a 'Configuração de Análise de Candles' section. This section must contain: (1) a read-only informational row labeled 'Motor de análise' showing the value 'Local (sem API externa)'; (2) the existing AI sensitivity slider labeled 'Sensibilidade da IA'; (3) the existing default timeframe selector (M1/M5/M10) labeled 'Timeframe padrão'; (4) the existing daily operation limit selector labeled 'Operações por dia'. All labels must be in Portuguese (Brazil). Keep all other existing settings (theme toggle, notifications) unchanged.

quotes: deixa apenas configuração de analise de candle

acceptanceCriteria: No Gemini API key field or section appears in Settings
A 'Configuração de Análise de Candles' section header is visible
Read-only row shows 'Motor de análise: Local (sem API externa)'
AI sensitivity, timeframe, and daily limit controls remain functional
Theme toggle and notifications settings remain unchanged

constraints: Do not make any external HTTP requests during chart analysis — all analysis must run entirely client-side using Canvas pixel data
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve existing analysis history storage in backend/main.mo — stored analyses must remain retrievable
Preserve daily operation management system (getDailyOperationProgress, setDailyOperationLimit) in the backend
nonGoals: Integrating any new external AI or API provider
Adding a Gemini API key or any API key configuration field
Modifying the Analysis History list layout beyond signal display updates
Changing the backend analysis storage schema beyond removing API key functions
